MATTER ARGUED, COURT ORDER MR. TAAFE, COUNSEL FOR BAY AREA AIR QUALITY MANAGEMENT DISTRICT TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER CONTAINING THE FOLLOWING: 1) THE COURT FINDS THAT THE REVISED OZONE ATTAINMENT PLAN IS A NEW PLAN AND THUS THE FAIR ARGUMENT STANDARD IS APPLIED TO DETERMINE WHETHER AN EIR SHOULD HAVE BEEN PREPARED. 2)THE COURT GRANTS THE WRIT AS TO THE ISSUE OF THE PREPARATION OF THE EIR BUT LIMITS THE REMEDY TO THE PREPARATION OF AN EIR BY RESPONDENTS ON ONLY TWO OF THE MEASURES IN THE PLAN SS-13 AND SS-14 AND THE SUBSEQUENT RULE MAKINGS ASSOCIATED W/THESE CONTROL MEASURES. PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE & COMPLAINT FOR DECLARATORY AND INJUNCTIVE RELIEF, AND MOTION TO STRIKE CERTAIN SECTIONS OF RESPONDENTS' FEBRUARY 18, 2003 SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF AND DECLARATION: COUNSEL APPEARED. MATTER ARGUED, COURT GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART PETITIONERS' WRIT OF ADMINSTRATIVE MANDAMUS. COURT ADOPTED RESPONDENTS' PROPOSED ORDER REGARDING CEQA CLAIMS. THE COURT FOUND THAT RESPONDENTS HAD VIOLATED HEALTH & SAFETY CODE 40233. THE COURT ORDERS RESPONDENTS, WITHIN 60 DAYS TO DEVELOP A PLAN THAT WILL REDUCE VOC EMISSIONS BY AN ADDITIONAL 26 TONS A DAY. THE COURT DID NOT VACATE THE 2001 OZONE ATTAINMENT PLAN OR THE TCM PLAN. THE COURT FOUND THAT THE CALIFORNIA AIR RESOURCES BOARD DID NOT CHANGE OR AMEND THE OZONE ATTAINMENT PLAN. PETITIONER WAS AWARDED FEES AND COSTS UPON SUBSEQUENT MOTION. PETITIONER TO PREPARE STATEMENT OF DECISION. ALL ADMINISTRATIVE RECCORDS WERE TO RETURN TO RESPONDENT. $90.00 PER DIEM FEES TO BE PAID BY PETITIONERS. RESPONDENTS WERE EXEMPT BECAUSE THEY WERE GOVERNMENT AGENCIES. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II/RS; CLERK: C. LI; REPORTER: PATRICIA M. EVEZICH, CSR #1193 (503)

To Strike Certain Sections Of Respondents' February 18, 2003 Supplemental Brief And Declaraton Of Brian Bunger In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Apr-24-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Petition for Writ of Mandate & Complaint for Declaratory and Injunctive Relief, and Motion to Strike Certain Sections of Respondents' February 18, 2003 Supplemental Brief and Declaration:
Appearance: Suma Peesapati, (510)302-0430x204 and Marc Chytilo, (805)682-0585 for petitioners; John Davidson, 703-5525 for Calif. Air Resource Control Board, Daniel J. Taafee, 357-1940 for Bay Area Air Quality Managment District, respondent; Tina Thomas, (916)443-2745 for respondent Metropolitan Transportation Commission.
Ruling: matter argued, Court granted in part and denied in part petitioners' writ of adminstrative mandamus. Court adopted respondents' proposed order regarding CEQA Claims. The court found that respondents had violated Health & Safety Code 40233. The court orders respondents, within 60 days to develop a plan that will reduce VOC emissions by an additional 26 tons a day. The court did not vacate the 2001 Ozone Attainment Plan or the TCM Plan. The court found that the California Air Resources Board did not change or amend the Ozone Attainment Plan. Petitioner was awarded fees and costs upon subsequent motion. Petitioner to prepare statement of decision. All administrative reccords were to return to Respondent. $90.00 per diem fees to be paid by petitioners. Respondents were exempt because they were government agencies. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II/RS; CLERK: C. LI; REPORTER: PATRICIA M. EVEZICH, CSR #1193 (503)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-01-2003 Continued To Jun-13-03 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

To Strike Certain Sections Of Respondents' February 18, 2003 Supplemental Brief And Declaraton Of Brian Bunger In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Apr-04-2003 Continued To Apr-24-03 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 503

Petitioner'S Petition For Writ Of Mandate And Complaint For Declaratory And Injunctive Relief
Off Calendar Mar-13-2003 Text Ruling

To Strike Certain Sections Of Respondents' February 18, 2003 Supplemental Brief And Declaraton Of Brian Bunger In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Mar-13-2003 Continued To Apr-04-03 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 503
MINI MINUTES: Petition for Writ of Mandate & Complaint for Declaratory and Injunctive Relief, and Motion to Strike Certain Sections of Respondents' February 18, 2003 Supplemental Brief and Declaration:
Appearance: Suma Peesapati, (510)302-0430x204 and Marc Chytilo, (805)682-0585 for petitioners; John Davidson, 703-5525 for Calif. Air Resource Control Board, Daniel J. Taafee, 357-1940 for Bay Area Air Quality Managment District, respondent; Tina Thomas, (916)443-2745 for respondent Metropolitan Transportation Commission.
Ruling: matter argued, court order Mr. Taafe, counsel for Bay Area Air Quality Management District to prepare proposed order containing the following: 1) The court finds that the Revised Ozone Attainment Plan is a new plan and thus the fair argument standard is applied to determine whether an EIR should have been prepared. 2)The court grants the writ as to the issue of the preparation of the EIR but limits the remedy to the preparation of an EIR by Respondents on only two of the measures in the Plan SS-13 and SS-14 and the subsequent rule makings associated w/these control measures.
Next hearing set 4-4-03 at 1:30 p.m. Respondent to prepare proposed order and memo of no more than 10 pages supporting the decision by 3-21-03, petitioner's 10 page objections due 3-28-03, respondent's 5 page reply by 4-1-03.
Also at the 4-4-03 hearing, the court will hear arguments regarding petitioners' assertions that respondents violated Health & Safety Code 40233, and that the Calif. Air Resources Board violated state law by making unauthorized changes to the Ozone Attainment Plan.
At 6:45 p.m., the matter concluded. $90.00 per diem fees to be paid by petitioners. Respondents were exempt because they were government agencies.
